task_completion.description: Import the dataset and configure it as a monthly time series starting in January 1949.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Gretl main window showing the 'airline_passengers' dataset loaded. The data information should indicate 144 observations, with a monthly periodicity (12/year) starting at 1949:01.

task_completion.description: Create a log-transformed variable for the passenger count.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new variable (e.g., 'l_passengers', 'log_passengers', or similar) appears in the variable list in the main Gretl window, indicating the log transformation was applied.

task_completion.description: Estimate an OLS regression using the log-transformed passenger count as the dependent variable, with a time trend and seasonal dummy variables as regressors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A model output window showing OLS estimates. The dependent variable must be the log-transformed passenger count. The independent variables must include a time trend (e.g., 'time') and exactly 11 seasonal dummy variables (e.g., 'dm2' through 'dm12'). The coefficient for the time trend should be approximately 0.010.

task_completion.description: Conduct a joint F-test for the significance of the seasonal dummy variables.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A test window or output section showing an F-test (often via 'Omit variables' or 'Linear restrictions' in Gretl) specifically testing the 11 seasonal dummy variables. The output should show an F-statistic of approximately 59.05.

task_completion.description: Save the regression results and the F-test output to the specified text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog pointing to '/home/ga/Documents/gretl_output/' with the filename 'seasonal_analysis.txt', a text editor showing the saved output, or a terminal/file manager confirming the file's creation at the exact path.

integrity.description: The agent used the Gretl GUI to perform the analysis rather than running a pre-written script.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with Gretl's menus (e.g., Data -> Dataset structure, Add -> Logs, Model -> Ordinary Least Squares) and dialog boxes.

integrity.description: The results shown in the output file or windows are generated by the software, not manually typed.
integrity.visual_evidence: The formatting of the OLS regression and F-test matches standard Gretl output, including standard errors, t-ratios, p-values, and R-squared values that are mathematically consistent.

integrity.description: The output is saved to the exact requested path.
integrity.visual_evidence: The save dialog or file manager explicitly shows the path '/home/ga/Documents/gretl_output/seasonal_analysis.txt' rather than a generic or default location.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset contains 144 monthly observations from 1949 to 1960. The OLS regression of the log-transformed passenger count on a time trend and seasonal dummies must use exactly 11 seasonal dummy variables to avoid the dummy variable trap (perfect multicollinearity). This regression should yield a time trend coefficient of approximately 0.010069 (acceptable range: 0.008 to 0.012). The joint F-test on these 11 seasonal dummies should yield an F-statistic of approximately F(11, 131) = 59.05 with a p-value < 1e-16.
